steps.title: Identify the preservation gap
steps.content: The passage says Indigenous songs can carry ecological information, such as Warlpiri songs about wild edible seeds and Temiar songs representing landforms and landmarks through melody and pitch.

steps.title: Include song-only expressions
steps.content: Language-preservation efforts have typically focused on spoken language, but some expressions in Indigenous languages appear only in songs. To secure a comprehensive range of information, preservation efforts must include those song forms.

steps.title: Reject narrower responses
steps.content: Choice A makes an unsupported claim about songs mostly using spoken phrases, choice B narrows the issue to ecological expressions, and choice C shifts to analyzing musical similarities instead of preserving language content. The answer is D.
